config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society works best when individual rights are protected first, because once you let “society” override personal rights too easily, it can be used to justify abuse by those in power. In my view, the common good matters, but it should be pursued through laws that respect dignity, freedom, and equal treatment for each person. From my perspective, a strong society is built by protecting people, not by sacrificing them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Saya setuju bahwa masyarakat punya kepentingan bersama—seperti keselamatan publik, kesehatan, dan ketertiban—tetapi kepentingan itu seharusnya dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan. Kalau hak individu dikorbankan terlalu cepat demi “kebaikan bersama,” yang sering terjadi justru kelompok lemah yang paling dirugikan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil tetap mengejar kepentingan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement: in my view, society should not automatically come before the individual, because that easily becomes a excuse for officials or majorities to ignore personal dignity and basic freedoms. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure people, silence criticism, or treat minority voices as less important, so I am very cautious about giving society priority in a broad way. Of course, individual rights are not absolute, because public safety and order do matter, but those limits must be narrow, fair, and clearly justified. A healthy society is not built by sacrificing the individual too quickly; it is built by protecting people while balancing the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat terlalu sering alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak individu. Memang benar ada keadaan seperti keselamatan publik, kesehatan, dan ketertiban yang bisa membatasi kebebasan tertentu, tetapi batas itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dipakai sebagai kartu bebas oleh pemerintah atau mayoritas. Saya juga setuju dengan kekhawatiran bahwa kelompok yang lemah biasanya paling dulu dirugikan ketika hak pribadi dikalahkan atas nama kepentingan bersama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah, melainkan yang menjaga hak individu sambil mengejar kebaikan umum secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “society first” can become a cover for silencing criticism or pressuring minorities. The common good matters, but if individual rights are treated as secondary by default, the people with the least power usually pay the price first. Public safety and order can justify limits, but only when those limits are narrow, transparent, and genuinely necessary. For me, a strong society is not one that asks individuals to surrender too much, but one that protects them while building the public good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, memberi prioritas pada kepentingan masyarakat di atas hak individu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, terutama untuk keselamatan publik, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi saya tidak percaya itu boleh dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk mengalahkan hak dasar orang. Saya juga melihat bahwa ketika hak individu dikorbankan atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” kelompok yang paling lemah biasanya yang paling cepat dirugikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu mencari kepentingan umum dengan batas yang jelas dan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
